articles.title: Oil steadies as markets await outcome of Trump-Xi talks amid Iran war concerns
articles.description: Oil prices edged higher as investors awaited the outcome of ongoing talks between Donald Trump and Xi Jinping in Beijing, while concerns over the Iran war, Strait of Hormuz disruptions and persistent US inflation kept energy markets on edge

articles.title: Havana faces up to 22-hour blackouts as Cuba runs out of diesel, fuel oil
articles.description: Only a single large oil tanker, the Russian-flagged Anatoly Kolodkin, has delivered crude oil to Cuba since December, providing temporary relief to the island in April

articles.title: India Bans Sugar Exports Till September 2026 Amid Domestic Supply Concerns
articles.description: The ban will not apply to sugar exports to the European Union and the United States.

articles.title: Strong cues from GIFT Nifty point to positive start for D-Street
articles.description: Indian stock markets are set for a wider trading range ahead. High Brent crude prices and a falling rupee are key concerns for investors. Foreign institutional investor outflows are expected to limit market gains. The final phase of the Q4FY26 earnings season and specific policy support will offer stock and sector-specific opportunities.

articles.title: The expanding arc of collateral damage to the Indian economy
articles.description: West Asia conflict is becoming a multi-vector test of India’s resilience

articles.title: Stocks in news: HAL, TMPV, Bharti Airtel, Anant Raj, Zydus Life, Tata Motors
articles.description: Indian markets experienced a volatile yet stable session after a sharp sell-off, with the Nifty showing resilience. Key companies like HAL, Tata Motors, and Bharti Airtel are in focus due to quarterly results and news developments. Zydus Lifesciences is nearing a US acquisition, while Maruti Suzuki commits to green logistics.

articles.title: Why is market rising today? Sensex zooms over 800 points, Nifty above 23,650. Key drivers behind surge
articles.description: Indian stock markets are experiencing a significant rally. The BSE Sensex and Nifty 50 indices have climbed over 1 percent each. This surge has added over Rs 3 lakh crore to the market capitalization of BSE-listed companies. The markets are extending gains for a second consecutive session. This positive movement is occurring despite ongoing global and domestic challenges.

articles.title: Max Estates targets Rs 1,200 crore revenue from the next phase of Gurugram project
articles.description: Max Estates aims for Rs 1,200 crore revenue from its new project, The Terraces, in Gurugram. This development is part of Estate 361, a large project designed for all age groups. The Terraces offers residences starting at Rs 2.4 crore. The project emphasizes sustainability and includes amenities for hybrid workers.

articles.title: India bans sugar exports until September 2026 amid supply concerns: Report
articles.description: India Business News: The Centre on Wednesday prohibited sugar exports with immediate effect till September 30 or until further orders, as the government moved to curb dome.

articles.title: Global Market Today: Asian stocks, US futures climb on tech optimism
articles.description: Asian stocks advanced, mirroring Wall Street's record highs driven by a tech surge and strong corporate earnings. Investors are closely watching the US-China summit and geopolitical developments in Iran, while inflation concerns fuel bets on Federal Reserve rate hikes next year.

articles.title: Gadkari opts for bus travel, cuts convoy size by 50%
articles.description: Nagpur: Union minister for road transport and highways Nitin Gadkari has decided to travel by bus during his May 14-15 visit to Pune, Satara and Solap.

articles.title: Maha govt halves ministers’ convoys
articles.description: Nagpur: Maharashtra govt on Wednesday ordered immediate reduction in the number of vehicles in the convoys of chief minister Devendra Fadnavis and all.

articles.title: Tripura govt orders fuel conservation, 50% staff to WFH on alternate weeks
articles.description: The Tripura government has mandated 50% of Group C and D staff to work from home on alternate weeks. The move follows PM Modi's appeal to conserve fuel and is part of new austerity measures to reduce government expenditure amid the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: Why Singapore’s fuel supply holds steady but not prices, and when cost pressures may ease
articles.description: The global chokepoint in the Strait of Hormuz is having a ripple effect on everyday people and everyday life — from drivers to school bus operators, from air travel to grocery shopping. CNA’s Talking Point dives into the rising fuel prices.

articles.title: Commentary: Why executive condominiums still offer a valid alternative for the 'sandwich' class
articles.description: Executive condominiums remain relevant for the "sandwich" middle class, though the government will need to keep a close watch on prices, says NUS Business School’s Sing Tien Foo.

articles.title: Global wine consumption falls as inflation bites and younger consumers drink less
articles.description: Wine drinking fell worldwide in 2025, with experts saying producers must adapt to changing lifestyles, economic pressure and rising demand for no- and low-alcohol options.

articles.title: LDA puts 475 properties worth ₹3,000 cr on e-auction
articles.description: LDA chairman Prathamesh Kumar said applicants can take part in the auction after depositing 10% of the reserve price as earnest money deposit (EMD). He said residential plots ranging from 25 square metres to 300 square metres have been offered in key locations across the city.

articles.title: AMC allots 102 plots for parking to ease traffic woes

articles.title: Uttarakhand CM Dhami urges energy saving, virtual meetings at BJP camp
articles.description: Uttarakhand CM Pushkar Singh Dhami urged BJP workers to conserve energy via virtual meetings and less travel, following PM Modi's appeal. He plans cabinet guidelines, while other BJP-ruled states also announced measures to cut vehicle use.

articles.title: Gujarat sees 20% surge in coconut output, reaches 26 crore annually
articles.description: Gujarat's tender coconut production has increased by nearly 20% over two years, reaching 26 crore coconuts annually. The government is promoting cultivation with subsidies and training, while farmers are using innovative methods to tackle pests.

articles.title: Pass % Dips, But Delhi Stays Ahead Of Class
articles.description: Delhi CBSE Class 12 results 2025: overall pass rate 92.1% (Delhi region 95.2%), above national 85.2% despite dip; girls outperform boys.

articles.title: Most married couples are leaving Social Security money on the table — here's how to claim it all
articles.description: Social Security benefits for married couples are becoming one of America’s biggest retirement conversations in 2026. The average dual-income retired couple now receives nearly $50,000 yearly in Social Security payments. Yet millions still lose thousands because they claim too early or misunderstand spousal benefit rules. Rising inflation, healthcare costs, and retirement pressure have pushed Social Security retirement income into the center of household survival planning.

articles.title: Why India just slapped a 15% tariff on gold and why it probably won't work
articles.description: India has raised gold and silver import tariffs to 15% as rising bullion prices and Iran war-linked oil pressures strain the current account and forex reserves.

articles.title: RSSB releases revised 2026 examination schedule for Clerk, Forester and Supervisor recruitment examinations
articles.description: The Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) has released a revised examination calendar for 2026, updating dates for key recruitment examinations conducted under the CET framework. The Supervisor Women Direct Recruitment Examination-2026 has been rescheduled to July 24, 2026, while the Forester and Clerk Grade-II/Junior Assistant examinations will be held as previously announced. RSSB stated that all other examinations listed in the earlier calendar issued on June 03, 2025 will continue without changes. Candidates have been advised to check the official website regularly.

articles.title: Quick-commerce firms push deeper into smaller cities as metro growth slows
articles.description: Amazon and Flipkart are expanding dark-store networks beyond major urban markets as competition and saturation pressure profitability in metros

articles.title: Government hikes paddy MSP by ₹72 to ₹2,441/qtl; sunflower seed sees steepest rise at ₹622
articles.description: Government raises paddy MSP by ₹72 to ₹2,441/qtl, with significant increases for sunflower seed and other crops to support farmers.

articles.title: Global gas markets stay calm as Hormuz disruption shifts winter risks
articles.description: Global LNG prices remain contained despite Hormuz tensions as Asian utilities switch to coal and Europe focuses on rebuilding winter gas storage.

articles.title: Panic At Chandni Chowk: Will PM Modi’s ‘No Gold’ Call Cripple Delhi’s Iconic Jewellery Hub?
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s appeal to halt gold purchases has sent shockwaves through India’s $80 billion gems and jewellery industry. With gold imports hitting a record $72 billion in fiscal year 2026-a 24% surge the government is prioritizing the stabilization of the rupee and the current account deficit over luxury consumption. However, the move has sparked deep anxiety among jewellers in hubs like Delhi’s Chandni Chowk and Mumbai’s Zaveri Bazaar. Industry leaders warn of a looming crisis for the lakhs of workers whose livelihoods depend on the sector, urging the government to consult stakeholders before implementing restrictive curbs.

articles.title: No work from home mandate, no import curbs planned: Centre on PM Modi’s austerity call
articles.description: As per CNBC-TV18, sources said the Prime Minister's remarks were only an appeal and no mandatory restrictions are under consideration at this stage

articles.title: Indian regulators oppose Pernod Ricard's liquor plea, firm says business 'hopelessly fettered'
articles.description: Indian regulators are opposing Pernod Ricard's latest court attempt to regain its Delhi liquor license. The French company's license was rejected due to serious allegations of collusion with retailers to boost market share. Pernod denies wrongdoing, but the ban has impacted its business for three years. The Delhi government cites public interest and regulatory risk in its opposition.

articles.title: Anduril raises $5 billion as defense tech valuation climbs to $61 billion
articles.description: Anduril Industries secured $5 billion in funding, boosting its valuation to $61 billion as investors back autonomous defense manufacturing and AI military software.

articles.description: The company had posted a consolidated net profit of ₹697.51 crore in the corresponding quarter of the preceding fiscal
